assertions.text: Identifies Google batchexecute as the RPC protocol (not REST)
assertions.evidence: Line 15: 'It uses Google's batchexecute RPC protocol — not a public REST API.' Line 178: 'This is not REST, not GraphQL, not gRPC-Web. It is Google batchexecute.'

assertions.text: Mentions rpc/ subpackage with encoder.py + decoder.py + types.py
assertions.evidence: Lines 454-458: 'core/rpc/' directory with 'types.py' (RPCMethod enum, URL constants), 'encoder.py' (encode_rpc_request, build_request_body), 'decoder.py' (strip_prefix, parse_chunks, extract_result).

assertions.text: Describes the triple-nested array encoding (f.req=[[[rpc_id, params, null, "generic"]]])
assertions.evidence: Line 213-216: 'The triple-nested structure is: [[[ rpc_id, json.dumps(params), null, "generic" ]]]'. Also encoder.py code at line 520-521 shows exact implementation.

assertions.text: Describes response decoding (strip )]}'  prefix + length-prefixed chunks + double-JSON)
assertions.evidence: Lines 226-228: 'After stripping the anti-XSSI prefix and parsing length-prefixed chunks, extract the wrb.fr entry...inner value is a JSON string requiring a second json.loads() call.' Full decoder code at lines 544-589 implements all three steps.

assertions.text: Supports 3 login methods (Playwright + CDP/from-browser + manual/cookies-json)
assertions.evidence: Lines 364-379: 'Method 1: auth login (Playwright)', 'Method 2: auth login --from-browser (CDP)', 'Method 3: auth login --cookies-json <file> (manual fallback)'. All three are described in detail with code.

assertions.text: Mentions x-same-domain: 1 header as critical
assertions.evidence: Line 249: 'x-same-domain: 1 — mandatory, Google rejects requests without it'. Line 1288-1291: 'Google's batchexecute endpoint verifies the presence of x-same-domain: 1 header...Without it, requests are rejected with 400 or 403.' Also in critical headers table at line 1346.

assertions.text: Extracts both CSRF token (SNlM0e) AND session ID (FdrFJe) from page HTML
assertions.evidence: Lines 243-246: 'Two additional tokens must be extracted from page HTML each session: SNlM0e — CSRF token used as at= in request body, FdrFJe — Session ID used as f.sid= in URL parameters'. Code at line 135: 'WIZ_global_data (source of SNlM0e CSRF token and FdrFJe session ID)'.

assertions.text: Has auto-retry on 401 with token refresh
assertions.evidence: Lines 401-406: 'When a batchexecute call returns 401 or 403, the client: 1. Re-fetches...with current cookies, 2. Parses new SNlM0e and FdrFJe from the HTML, 3. Updates the in-memory token cache and auth.json, 4. Retries the failed request once.' Code in client.py at lines 658-663 implements this.

assertions.text: Identifies independent command modules that can be built in parallel (Phase 4)
assertions.evidence: Lines 679-692: 'Step 2 (parallel subagents): All command modules are independent of each other. Each only imports from core/. Dispatch simultaneously:' followed by table of 6 agents for notebooks, sources, chat, notes, audio, and auth_cmds.

assertions.text: Mentions dispatching parallel subagents for command files
assertions.evidence: Lines 679-692: 'Dispatch simultaneously:' with Agent 1 through Agent 6 table. Also lines 958-971 describe parallel subagent dispatch for test writing: 'Agent 1...Agent 2...Agent 3...Agents 1 and 2 can start in parallel immediately.'

assertions.text: Mentions TEST.md two-part structure (plan before code, results appended after)
assertions.evidence: Line 756: 'This document would be written to cli_web/notebooklm/tests/TEST.md BEFORE any test code.' Lines 1065-1066: 'TEST.md Part 2 is APPENDED (not overwritten) to the existing Part 1 document'. Line 1379: 'TEST.md has both parts'.

assertions.text: Tests must FAIL (not skip) without auth — mentions pytest.fail() or equivalent
assertions.evidence: Lines 838-846: 'def require_auth():' fixture uses 'pytest.fail("Auth not configured...")' and 'pytest.fail("Auth incomplete...")'. Line 1379: 'E2E live tests FAIL without auth'.

assertions.text: Includes realistic workflow scenarios (CRUD round-trip, auth flow, etc.)
assertions.evidence: Lines 874-938: Six detailed scenarios — 'Scenario 1: Notebook CRUD round-trip', 'Scenario 2: Source and chat workflow', 'Scenario 3: Notes CRUD round-trip', 'Scenario 4: Audio overview generation', 'Scenario 5: Auth token refresh', 'Scenario 6: Bulk operations'.

assertions.text: Has an end-user smoke test in Phase 8 (auth login -> auth status -> real API call)
assertions.evidence: Lines 1110-1202: Phase 8 smoke test with Step 1 (auth login via Playwright), Step 2 (auth status showing 'Live validation: OK'), Step 3 (notebooks list verifying real JSON), Step 4 (CRUD smoke: create, list, add source, chat ask, create note, delete).

assertions.text: Explicitly states pipeline is NOT done until smoke test passes with real data
assertions.evidence: Lines 1124: 'Every step must succeed. If any step fails, the pipeline is NOT complete.' Lines 1216-1224: 'The pipeline is complete ONLY when ALL of the following are true:' followed by checklist including 'notebooks list returns real data from the live API'.

assertions.text: Identifies Google batchexecute as the RPC protocol (not REST)
assertions.evidence: Lines 147-148: 'Confirmed Protocol: Google batchexecute RPC. NotebookLM does not use REST JSON or gRPC over HTTP/2 with protobuf. It uses Google's internal batchexecute mechanism.'

assertions.text: Mentions rpc/ subpackage with encoder.py + decoder.py + types.py
assertions.evidence: Lines 737-740: 'http/rpc/' directory with 'encoder.py' (encode_rpc_request, build_request_body, build_url_params), 'decoder.py' (decode_batchexecute_response, parse_chunks), 'methods.py' (RPC_METHODS registry). Note: file is named 'methods.py' not 'types.py' but serves the same purpose.

assertions.text: Describes the triple-nested array encoding (f.req=[[[rpc_id, params, null, "generic"]]])
assertions.evidence: Lines 173-181: 'When decoded, f.req is: [[["CCqFvf", "[[\"My Notebook\"]]", null, "generic"]]]'. Also: 'The outer structure is [[[rpc_id, payload_string, null, "generic"]]].' Code at line 566 implements this.

assertions.text: Describes response decoding (strip )]}' prefix + length-prefixed chunks + double-JSON)
assertions.evidence: Lines 186-229: Full response format description with ')]}' prefix, byte_count/json_chunk alternation, wrb.fr entries. Code at lines 201-229 implements decode_batchexecute_response with strip, chunk parsing, and json.loads(entry[2]) double-decode.

assertions.text: Supports 3 login methods (Playwright + CDP/from-browser + manual/cookies-json)
assertions.evidence: Only Playwright login is described (lines 331-379: 'Recommended Approach: Playwright Browser Login'). There is no --from-browser/CDP login method and no --cookies-json manual import method. The file mentions only one login command at line 593: 'login — Playwright browser login'.

assertions.text: Mentions x-same-domain: 1 header as critical
assertions.evidence: Line 501: '"X-Same-Domain": "1"' in CHROME_HEADERS dict. However, it is listed as just one of many headers without explicit explanation of why it's critical. Borderline but present.

assertions.text: Extracts both CSRF token (SNlM0e) AND session ID (FdrFJe) from page HTML
assertions.evidence: Lines 320-324: 'SNlM0e — CSRF token, embedded in window.WIZ_global_data' and 'FdrFJe — session/build ID, extracted from the same object'. Code at lines 395-419 implements extraction with regex patterns.

assertions.text: Has auto-retry on 401 with token refresh
assertions.evidence: Lines 574-583: 'CSRF Token Refresh...The client should refresh automatically on AuthError'. Lines 1262-1280: test_csrf_auto_refresh test corrupts token and verifies auto-refresh. Also line 965: 'Auth refresh triggered automatically on first AuthError'.

assertions.text: Identifies independent command modules that can be built in parallel (Phase 4)
assertions.evidence: Lines 819-854: 'The codebase has a clear dependency graph that allows four parallel tracks:' with Track A through E. Track C lists all API modules (notebooks, sources, chat, notes, artifacts, research, sharing, settings) as parallelizable.

assertions.text: Mentions dispatching parallel subagents for command files
assertions.evidence: The file describes parallel 'tracks' and 'sprint parallelism' (line 854) but never mentions dispatching subagents. It uses architectural dependency tracks, not agent-based parallel dispatch. No mention of 'subagent' or 'dispatch' anywhere in the file.

assertions.text: Mentions TEST.md two-part structure (plan before code, results appended after)
assertions.evidence: No mention of TEST.md anywhere in the file (grep returned 0 results). The testing section describes test files (test_rpc_encoder.py, etc.) but not the TEST.md two-part document structure.

assertions.text: Tests must FAIL (not skip) without auth — mentions pytest.fail() or equivalent
assertions.evidence: Line 1014: Uses 'pytest.mark.skipif(not os.environ.get("NOTEBOOKLM_AUTH_JSON"), reason="...")' — this SKIPS tests without auth, it does not FAIL them. The assertion requires pytest.fail(), not skipif.

assertions.text: Includes realistic workflow scenarios (CRUD round-trip, auth flow, etc.)
assertions.evidence: Lines 1025-1035: E2E tests include 'Create notebook -> appears in list -> get by ID -> rename -> delete', 'Add URL source -> wait=True completes -> delete', 'Ask a question -> get non-empty answer'. Smoke test at lines 1364-1462 has full lifecycle tests. However these are less explicitly named 'scenarios' than the with_skill version.

assertions.text: Has an end-user smoke test in Phase 8 (auth login -> auth status -> real API call)
assertions.evidence: Lines 1317-1462: Section 10 'Production Smoke Test' with test_01_auth_valid (auth tokens + list notebooks), test_02_notebook_created, test_04_source_added, test_05_chat_works. The flow covers auth verification -> real API calls.

assertions.text: Explicitly states pipeline is NOT done until smoke test passes with real data
assertions.evidence: Lines 1483-1496: 'The CLI is production-ready when all of the following are true:' with 10 criteria including 'all 12 tests pass against live API', 'notebooklm auth check --test returns exit code 0'. While it says 'production-ready' instead of 'pipeline NOT done', the intent is equivalent.
